prompt: Write me a resume. I was a warehouse associate at Amazon for 3 years. I have forklift certification and OSHA 10. I'm applying for a production supervisor role at Emerson Electric. Here's the JD: Production Supervisor — oversee daily production operations, manage team of 12, ensure safety compliance, track KPIs, coordinate with quality control.
quality_checks: Resume includes Professional Summary, Core Skills, Experience, Education, Certifications
Core Skills mirrors JD keywords: production operations, team management, safety compliance, KPIs, quality control
Forklift certification and OSHA 10 appear in Certifications section
Experience bullets use action verbs + quantified results
No fabricated experience or employers
ATS-compatible format: no tables, no columns, standard headings

prompt: I have an interview tomorrow for a CNA position at a nursing home. Help me prepare.
quality_checks: Produces exactly 5 questions
Each question has STAR-format answer structure
Questions are CNA/healthcare-specific (patient care, difficult patients, safety, teamwork, stress)
Coaching tips mention reliability and safety awareness
Includes questions to ask the interviewer

prompt: I just got released from DOC last week. I did 2 years. I learned welding inside — got my AWS certification. Help me find work.
quality_checks: Does NOT recommend disclosing conviction in any output
AWS welding certification listed prominently
Flags Fair Chance employers
Mentions Federal Bonding Program
Mentions WOTC as competitive advantage
Routes to Reentry Connect / OWD Reentry Specialist
Tone is empowering and forward-looking, not pitying

prompt: /casenote — Met with James T. today. He's a dislocated worker from the Boeing layoff in January. We reviewed his resume, made 4 job referrals to manufacturing positions, and referred him to Fast Track for CNC machinist training. Follow-up in 2 weeks.
quality_checks: Uses first name + last initial only (James T.)
Includes Service Type, Staff, Date, Summary
Lists specific services: resume review, 4 job referrals, training referral
Referral to Fast Track documented with reason
Follow-up date specified (2 weeks out)
No SSN, full birthdate, or sensitive PII
Professional, neutral language

prompt: They offered me $14/hour for medical assistant in St. Louis. Is that good?
quality_checks: Provides wage context with source (BLS, MERIC, or state data)
Indicates whether $14 is below/at/above market for the role and area
Includes negotiation framework (5-step process)
Mentions WOTC if applicable
Does NOT guarantee a specific outcome
Suggests getting offer in writing before accepting

prompt: Quick apply to this: Medical Records Clerk at BJC HealthCare. Requirements: high school diploma, medical terminology knowledge, EMR experience preferred, attention to detail, HIPAA compliance.
quality_checks: Produces 3-bullet resume summary
Produces 1-paragraph cover note
Produces short application email
All three mirror JD keywords: medical terminology, EMR, HIPAA, attention to detail
Each section is clearly labeled and separated

prompt: /pitch OJT to a local construction company that's never heard of it
quality_checks: Produces a conversational cold call script
Explains OJT wage reimbursement clearly (50-90%)
Mentions WIOA waiver for up to 90% reimbursement
Includes a closing question/call to action
Does not use unexplained acronyms
Appropriate for a phone conversation, not a letter

prompt: I want to become an RN but I only have a GED. What's the fastest path and how do I pay for it?
quality_checks: Maps stackable credential pathway: CNA → LPN → RN
Includes timeline estimate for each step
Lists funding sources: Fast Track, WIOA ITA, HEALS, Pell Grant
Mentions community college as primary provider
Does NOT promise eligibility for any program
Routes to Job Center for official enrollment
